steps.title: Identify what the blank requires
steps.content: The sentence needs two things: a possessive to show the photos belong to Ansel Adams (one photographer), and a plural noun because "many of the ______" signals multiple items. The correct form is singular possessive photographer's paired with plain plural photos (no apostrophe).

steps.title: Eliminate wrong choices and confirm the answer
steps.content:
• Choice A — photographer's early photo's: photo's is a singular possessive — it violates the rule that a plural noun takes no apostrophe. There is more than one photo, so the apostrophe is wrong. ✗
  • Choice B — photographers early photo's: photographers is a bare plural with no possessive apostrophe, failing to show the photos belong to Adams; photo's also wrongly treats the plural as a possessive. Two errors. ✗
  • Choice C — photographer's early photos: photographer's is correctly singular possessive (one person owns the photos); photos is correctly a simple plural with no apostrophe. Both rules satisfied. ✓
  • Choice D — photographers early photos: photographers lacks the apostrophe needed to show possession, so it fails to convey that the photos belong to Adams. ✗
That matches Choice C — photographer's early photos.
